name: CEO / Board Chair
description: Chief Executive Officers and Board Chairpersons at European corporations facing complex stakeholder environments — M&A, regulatory pressure, energy transition, political scrutiny, or crisis situations
painPoints: Business strategy faces unexpected resistance from stakeholders (regulators, investors, media, communities) after decisions are made
Strategy consultants deliver a deck and leave — no one owns the stakeholder dimension
Crisis situations escalate because stakeholder landscape was not mapped in advance
Board pressure to demonstrate responsible governance across all stakeholder groups
decisionCriteria: Senior partners personally involved — not junior consultants
Track record in high-stakes situations (crisis, M&A, regulatory)
Ability to integrate strategy and communications as one discipline
Political and regulatory access and insight
Confidentiality and discretion
skepticism: Has worked with large strategy firms and communications agencies before — skeptical that a boutique firm can deliver at their level. Needs to see real crisis/M&A track record and political access.
adaptedMessage: We make your business strategy stakeholder-proof — stress-tested against every angle before it faces scrutiny, with one clear recommendation backed by intelligence, not a set of options.

topObjection.objection: We already work with McKinsey/BCG for strategy and a communications agency for messaging — why do we need another advisor?
topObjection.response: Strategy firms analyse the business logic; communications agencies package the message. Neither stress-tests the strategy against the full stakeholder landscape. We sit in between — questioning whether the strategy will survive investor, regulatory, media, and community scrutiny before you're committed. The companies that fail don't fail on business logic — they fail on stakeholder acceptance.

name: Corporate Affairs / Communications Director
description: Heads of Corporate Affairs, Corporate Communications, or Public Affairs at large European organizations who manage stakeholder relations and need strategic depth beyond execution
painPoints: Internal strategy team makes decisions without considering stakeholder implications — communications team is brought in too late
Lack of data-driven stakeholder intelligence — relying on instinct and relationships alone
Need to elevate the communications function from 'execution' to 'strategic counsel'
Managing multiple concurrent stakeholder challenges (crisis, regulation, transformation) without senior external support
decisionCriteria: Strategic depth — not just execution capabilities
AI-driven analytics and stakeholder mapping tools
Senior advisors who can credibly counsel the CEO and board
Track record that demonstrates communications as a strategic function
Cultural fit — bold, direct, no-nonsense
skepticism: Worried about yet another agency that promises strategy but delivers press releases. Needs to see that Duke genuinely operates at the strategy level, not just communications execution.
adaptedMessage: We bring AI-driven stakeholder intelligence and strategic advisory that reveals blind spots your traditional analysis misses — elevating corporate affairs from execution to the boardroom.

topObjection.objection: How is this different from what our existing communications agency does?
topObjection.response: Most agencies execute messaging for a strategy someone else defined. We question the strategy itself through stakeholder lenses. Our AI-driven stakeholder mapping identified 3 opposition coalitions before they activated publicly for an energy company — something traditional communications research would never surface. We don't replace your agency; we make the strategy they're asked to communicate actually resilient.

name: General Counsel / M&A Lead
description: General Counsels, M&A Directors, and transaction leaders at European corporations managing deals, regulatory engagements, or litigation with significant stakeholder dimensions
painPoints: M&A transactions derailed by poor employee or media communications
Regulatory engagement that lacks strategic narrative — purely defensive
Litigation communications that damage the company's position rather than support it
Cross-border transactions requiring coordinated stakeholder management across jurisdictions
decisionCriteria: Proven M&A communications track record with measurable outcomes
Ability to coordinate across multiple jurisdictions and stakeholder groups simultaneously
Experience managing regulatory narratives
Confidentiality and legal-grade discretion
Speed of mobilization for time-sensitive transactions
skepticism: Legal professionals are naturally cautious with external advisors. Needs to see precise, measurable outcomes from comparable transactions and confidence that the team understands regulatory and legal constraints.
adaptedMessage: We manage transaction communications across every stakeholder group and jurisdiction — protecting deal timelines, employee retention, and regulatory outcomes with the precision legal teams expect.

topObjection.objection: Our law firm handles M&A communications — why do we need a separate advisor?
topObjection.response: Law firms manage legal disclosure obligations. We manage the strategic narrative across 8+ stakeholder groups — employees, media, analysts, regulators, partners, customers, shareholders, and political stakeholders — simultaneously across time zones. In a recent EUR 450M cross-border acquisition, that orchestration delivered 92% employee retention versus the 70-75% industry average. The legal work was impeccable; the communications work is what kept the people.

name: Government Affairs / Public Affairs Lead
description: Heads of Government Affairs or Public Affairs at companies facing regulatory pressure, legislative engagement, or political stakeholder challenges in the Netherlands and EU
painPoints: Proposed legislation threatening business model with limited political relationships in the Benelux/EU
Need to build government relations capability quickly in new markets
Difficulty translating business strategy into a credible regulatory narrative
Lack of political intelligence on coalition dynamics and legislative trajectories
decisionCriteria: Direct government access — people who have been inside the decision-making process
Understanding of Dutch and EU legislative processes
Ability to build relationships, not just lobby
Political intelligence quality and timeliness
Track record of policy influence
skepticism: Wary of advisors who claim political access but lack genuine relationships. Needs to see that Expert Partners actually sat at the decision-making table, not just adjacent to it.
adaptedMessage: Our Expert Partners include a former Minister of Economic Affairs and a former Intelligence Director — direct insight into how government decisions are actually made, not how outsiders think they work.

topObjection.objection: We need people who know the current government, not former officials from previous administrations.
topObjection.response: Political processes outlast individual administrations. Bas van 't Wout doesn't just know the players — he knows the machinery: how ministry positions form, how parliamentary committees build consensus, how coalition dynamics shape legislation. That structural knowledge, combined with maintained relationships, is what turned a technology platform's 5 policy positions into 3 provisions in final legislation. We don't just have access — we have understanding.
